run_summary: Portfolio holds four positions, all moderately profitable or flat. NTLA and PTCT have solid unrealized gains on intact 8-K catalysts with short horizons; both warrant HOLD pending next data. ALK has modest gain on Iran-deal fuel-relief thesis that remains plausible but unconfirmed (oil/fuel data needed). ALGN is underwater and newly flagged as OVERDONE—review shows the 5.02+7.01 pairing today is consistent with a managed succession, which argues against further downside, so HOLD pending Reg FD details. On new flags: AVAV re-entry fails the bar (re-entry warning, same Thursday earnings event, conf 4 but no NEW info beyond prior exit reasoning). BBIO and CALX are minor RATIONAL/OVERDONE conf-3 names without compelling setups. AEIS is a solid OVERDONE conf-3 fade (SMH sympathy on empty catalyst). AYI re-entry fails the bar (same XLI sympathy thesis that already broke). The day's most interesting flag is AVAV—but it is a recent loss-exit and re-entry discipline blocks it. Otherwise, a quiet discovery run with one fade candidate (AEIS) worth a small exploratory test.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
entries.catalyst: Apparent sympathy with SMH -3.97% sector dump (AVGO -3.67%, ORCL -2.58%, ONTO -5.9%, VECO -5.96%); no AEIS-specific 8-K or news.
entries.thesis: AEIS has no 8-K, no earnings imminent, and is classified as Industrial/Electrical Equipment yet derives substantial revenue from semiconductor capital equipment customers. SMH is down nearly 4% and ONTO/VECO are down 6% — AEIS is getting tarred with the same brush despite its more diversified end-market mix (also industrial, data center, medical). On 4.9x average volume with no news, this looks like a sympathy washout that retraces faster than the pure-play semicap names once the SMH downdraft stabilizes.
entries.what_kills: A late-breaking 8-K with a customer-loss disclosure or guide-down, sector weakness extending on confirmed cap-ex cuts from a major fab customer, or AEIS-specific negative sell-side note.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (4 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: OVERDONE conf-3: AEIS -7.21% today, -6.57% over 5 days on 4.88x volume with zero company-specific catalyst. SMH down 3.97%, ONTO -5.9%, VECO -5.96% — classic semicap sympathy wash. AEIS is Industrials/Electrical Equipment with diversified end-market (also data center, medical, not just semicon capital equipment), so it over-correlates to SMH on risk-off but decouples faster on recovery. No 8-K, no news, no earnings imminent. Catalyst is 'apparent sympathy with SMH downdraft; no AEIS-specific filing.' Setup is clean: (1) catalyst is sector beta only, no company deterioration, (2) volume spike on negative sentiment (4.88x) suggests forced liquidation rather than fundamental repricing, (3) AEIS' diversified end-markets historically mean it retraces faster than pure-plays when SMH stabilizes. Thesis: fading sympathy washout. What confirms: SMH stabilizes or bounces, AEIS bounces harder than ONTO/VECO. What kills: AEIS-specific 8-K with customer loss or guide-down, or sector weakness confirmed by major fab capex cuts. This is a true exploratory candidate—real catalyst (sector-sympathy fade on diversified end-market reprieve), clean setup, confidence 3, sized at 6% per two-tier rules. Will generate graded trade data on whether sympathy fades correlate with recover timing. Entering at market open.

red_team.weakest_link: The thesis assumes sympathy fades predictably and faster than pure-plays recover, but provides no historical data or mechanism for why AEIS specifically decouples on sector recovery.
red_team.critique: The bull case rests on a single bet: that AEIS will retrace faster than ONTO/VECO once SMH stabilizes, because AEIS has more diversified end-markets. But this is stated as pattern recognition, not as a causal mechanism with supporting evidence. The 4.88x volume spike on a -7.21% move is real and suggests forced selling, yet the same volume spike could equally indicate institutional capitulation that lasts longer if semiconductor capital equipment demand is genuinely rolling over (not just sentiment). No historical analysis is offered showing that diversified-end-market suppliers actually decouple faster than pure-plays in semiconductor downturns—this is the critical assumption and it is unvalidated. The setup is clean (no 8-K, no imminent earnings), but cleanness of setup is not the same as confidence in the recovery timing or magnitude.

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: 6/22 8-K with item 4.02 (non-reliance on prior financials) + 5.02 (officer departure); follow-up 6/25 8-K with another 5.02 + Reg FD disclosure; earnings 6/29.
entries.thesis: A 4.02 non-reliance filing is among the most severe 8-K codes — it explicitly tells investors prior financials cannot be relied on. Combined with 5.02 officer changes on the same filing AND a follow-up 5.02/7.01 filing the next trading day, this is bad-news bundling of the most concerning kind. Stock is down sharply over five days but only marginally green today; with earnings in 3 days, the print itself becomes a second leg risk (impairments, withdrawn guidance, audit committee disclosures). The 5-day move likely under-discounts what a restated-numbers earnings call typically delivers.
entries.what_kills: Restatement turns out to be narrow/technical (e.g., single non-cash reclassification), officer departure is the CFO being replaced by a stronger hire announced concurrently, or company pre-announces in-line operating metrics before the print.
entries.reasoning: Re-entry warning: you closed AVAV 3 days ago for a -4.75% realized loss on the identical thesis (4.02 non-reliance + 5.02 officer departure + earnings 6/29). The prior exit reasoning correctly noted that the 8-K's own 'what_kills' clause allows for a narrow restatement + planned transition scenario, which would dissipate the overhang quickly, and that entry conviction was insufficient given the -7.64% one-day move. Today's flag re-raises UNDERDONE conf-4 with the same 6/29 earnings catalyst. Re-entry discipline requires BOTH: (1) confidence >= 4 (✓ met: conf 4), AND (2) genuinely new information not present at exit. The new information here is: 3 additional days of data, which may have revealed scope specificity in the 6/22 8-K, OR buy-side accumulation, OR sell-side note that reframes risk. However, the flag itself does not cite new information beyond what the prior exit already analyzed. The flag repeats 'restatement severity underpriced' and 'earnings will deliver second-leg risk' — the same thesis that already resulted in a loss in one day. Without explicit new evidence (e.g., 'sell-side downgrades emerged since 6/24' or 'short interest surged'), re-entering 3 days after a loss on the same Thursday earnings event is performance-chasing into a binary headline risk. WATCH instead; if post-earnings data shows the restatement was indeed narrow and the stock stabilizes, re-entry becomes justified on confirmed new information.

entries.name: BridgeBio Pharma, Inc.
entries.catalyst: 8-K with annual-meeting-pattern items (5.02 director matters, 5.07 shareholder vote, 9.01 exhibits); broader XBI/XLV strength.
entries.thesis: 8-K items 5.02 + 5.07 + 9.01 is the classic annual meeting / director election / proxy-vote-results combination — administrative, not material. The barely-positive move on heavy volume against a strongly green XBI/XLV tape suggests the stock is mostly riding the sector rather than reacting to anything filing-specific. Magnitude appears appropriate to the catalyst (which is essentially nil at the company level).
entries.what_kills: 5.02 turns out to be an unexpected senior officer departure rather than routine board rotation, which would reframe the day's relative underperformance vs XBI as bearish.
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL conf-3 on routine 8-K items (5.02 director election, 5.07 shareholder vote, 9.01 exhibits) + sector tailwind (XBI up, XLV up). Flag thesis: magnitude appropriate, stock riding index/ETF rebalance rather than company-specific catalyst. Five-day move +5.99% on normal volume (1.17x average... wait, volume_multiple 4.74x is actually heavy). This contradicts the 'routine rebalance' interpretation — 4.74x volume on what the flag calls a nil-catalyst event is worth watching, not ignoring. However, the flag itself concludes this is likely index-driven and confidence is only 3. No conviction setup. Position sizing for a routine-governance name on sector beta would be exploratory at best, but the lack of a named company-specific catalyst and conf-3 rating argue against even a 6% entry. WATCH to see if this vol-spike has follow-up; if a surprising 5.02 detail emerges (unplanned departure), revisit.

entries.name: Acuity Inc.
entries.catalyst: Q4 earnings release filed via 8-K item 2.02
entries.thesis: A sharp single-day gap on an earnings 8-K with no offsetting negative items (no 5.02, no 4.02, no impairment) suggests a clean beat-and-raise. Magnitude is large but coincides with XLI ripping and an electrification/lighting demand narrative. Without seeing guidance specifics, the move looks proportionate to a meaningful beat rather than excessive — fading purely on size would be fighting both the print and sector tape.
entries.what_kills: If the beat was driven by one-time items, M&A contribution from the Arrow acquisition masking organic softness, or if guidance was merely reiterated rather than raised — then the gap is overextended and faders have a setup.
entries.reasoning: Re-entry warning: you closed AYI 12 days ago for +2.77% realized gain on OVERDONE conf-3 (XLI sympathy on CPI/Iran, weeks horizon). Prior exit reasoning noted: thesis horizon was weeks but move went NEGATIVE (-6.5% day of exit, -10.37% over 5 days), so direction reversed and conviction broke. Today's flag re-flags AYI RATIONAL conf-3 on Q4 earnings 8-K item 2.02 (clean beat-and-raise, single-item filing, no 5.02/4.02). Re-entry discipline: (1) confidence must be >= 4 (flag shows conf-3, so execution layer will auto-downgrade BUY to WATCH anyway), AND (2) new information. The new information IS real here — the Q4 earnings 8-K is a genuinely new event (filed 6/25, post-exit). The flag thesis is entirely different from the prior sympathy-fade thesis: it's now a beat-and-raise + XLI sector bid. However, re-entry discipline explicitly states conf-3 re-entries are downgraded by the execution layer and forfeits the trade. The flag's own confidence (3) is below the re-entry bar. Moreover, prior exit occurred because the name was DOWN when you expected sympathy to fade — it became real weakness. Today's flag doesn't address whether that fundamental deterioration was transient or structural. A clean earnings beat today would show it was transient, but entering at +17.58% over 5 days on a RATIONAL conf-3 (not a conviction event) risks buying the gap after a reprieve from deterioration. WATCH for post-earnings confirmation of the beat (do press release or guidance confirm strong FY outlook); if so, re-entry becomes viable at a lower conf-4 threshold on second-leg strength.

entries.name: Calix, Inc
entries.catalyst: Unknown — elevated volume without visible news
entries.thesis: CALX -3% today on 2.66x average volume — the volume spike is the interesting signal, not the price move. No 8-K, no scheduled earnings, no obvious peer drag (broadband-access equipment isn't part of the SMH wreckage). High-volume selling in a $2.3B name without identifiable catalyst often reflects fund-level repositioning that reverses when the seller is done. Tech-sector beta weak today provides cover but doesn't fully explain. Modest fade setup if next session shows volume normalizing.
entries.what_kills: BEAD/rural broadband funding cut announcement or major tier-1 telco capex revision — this would justify and extend the move
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE conf-3: CALX -2.96% today, -5.14% over 5 days on 2.66x volume with no visible catalyst (no 8-K, no earnings, no peer drag from SMH wreckage). Flag thesis: high-volume selling in small-cap without identifiable catalyst often reflects fund-level repositioning that reverses when seller is done. Fade setup if next session shows volume normalizing. Confidence is only 3, thesis is speculative (fund unwind), and no named catalyst URL. While the logic is sound (vol spike without news is often flow-driven), the lack of a specific trigger and low confidence argue against entry. WATCH for volume normalization + any customer news (BEAD/rural-broadband cuts would be the obvious risk). If volume reverts to normal and no news emerges over next 1-2 sessions, then a small fade becomes buyable; right now insufficient signal.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Sector beta to SMH +3.3% / TSM +5.3% rally; no company-specific news detected
entries.thesis: ONTO +8.5% on a day SMH is +3.3% and TSM +5.3% is a high-beta sympathy move within a sector tailwind. Volume only 1.18x average suggests this isn't a news-driven re-rate — it's beta. Five-day change is roughly flat, so this is a one-day catch-up rather than a trend break.
entries.what_kills: If an after-hours 8-K appears (large order win, guidance raise) or a sell-side note tied specifically to ONTO surfaces, this becomes UNDERDONE rather than RATIONAL.

entries.name: Repligen Corporation
entries.catalyst: Unknown — possible REGN/RGEN ticker confusion unwind given REGN FDA news, but unconfirmed
entries.thesis: RGEN -6.8% on 1.57x volume with XBI +1.9% is a meaningful divergence. No 8-K, no scheduled earnings. The Regeneron FDA PreCheck headline is for REGN (different company) — possible ticker confusion unwind if traders bought RGEN by mistake earlier and are now reversing, but I cannot confirm that mechanism from available data. Without a clear catalyst I can't articulate either side at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: If a customer-loss, guidance cut, or competitor (Danaher/Cytiva) win announcement is found, the move is justified and RATIONAL.

entries.name: Five Below, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — possible continuation of tariff/import-cost concern, but no specific catalyst in feed
entries.thesis: FIVE -5.4% on average volume with XLY +2.4% is a sharp relative-strength break (~8% underperformance). Five-day is -7.9%, so this is a continuation of weakness, not a one-day surprise. No 8-K, no earnings imminent. Possible tariff/import-cost narrative since FIVE is heavily China-sourced and a risk-on tape might be lifting names with better domestic exposure while leaving import-dependent names behind — but I can't anchor that at conf 3+ without a specific headline.
entries.what_kills: If DLTR/DG/OLLI are up with sector, FIVE-specific issue (channel check, mgmt commentary) is implied; if they're all down, it's a discount-retail narrative.

entries.name: JetBlue Airways Corporation
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no detectable catalyst in feed
entries.thesis: JBLU -6.2% is a notable divergence on a risk-on day with oil weakening (which should help airlines) and XLI +0.86%. Volume below average (0.9x), suggesting this isn't capitulation but possibly a forced seller or a single broker action. No 8-K, no earnings near. JBLU is a low-priced, high-short-interest, balance-sheet-stressed name where moves of this size are routine and often technical. Can't articulate either direction at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: If peer airlines (LUV, AAL, UAL, ALK) also down 3-5%, sector narrative; if JBLU alone, idiosyncratic (Spirit-related credit, route news, etc.).

entries.name: National Beverage Corp.
entries.catalyst: Earnings 7/1 after-hours; no other identified catalyst.
entries.thesis: Five-day move is meaningfully negative on heavy relative volume into an earnings print in 5 days, suggesting informed selling — but no 8-K and no public catalyst, and FIZZ is famously low-coverage with a controlling shareholder. Could be pre-earnings de-risking on weak channel checks (LaCroix shelf data) or could be illiquid retail flow. Cannot articulate either direction at conf 3+ without checking syndicated scanner data.
entries.what_kills: Volume turns out to be option-related or single-block; competitor sparkling water names show similar weakness suggesting category, not company.

entries.name: Manhattan Associates
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no scheduled earnings, no identified news.
entries.thesis: MANH popping strongly while XLK is down nearly 2% is highly unusual for a quality SaaS name — the relative move is the signal, but with no 8-K, no earnings, and no public news identified, I cannot determine whether this is a takeout rumor, analyst upgrade, customer win, or other. The magnitude warrants attention but I cannot construct a directional thesis at conf 3+ without identifying the catalyst.
entries.what_kills: Move reverses on Monday with no news identified, suggesting it was a technical or quant-flow artifact.

entries.name: Kratos Defense & Security
entries.catalyst: No 8-K identified; possible sympathy fade from AVAV restatement; possible relief bounce off five-day drawdown.
entries.thesis: Five-day weakness in KTOS while ITA is roughly flat suggests company-specific concern, but no 8-K and no clear public catalyst. Today's bounce on heavy volume could be a dead-cat bounce or the start of a relief rally. With AVAV (peer drone/defense name) just having filed a 4.02 restatement, there's a possibility of contagion fear in the unmanned-systems subspace that may or may not be justified for KTOS specifically.
entries.what_kills: KTOS files its own concerning 8-K; defense-spending news turns negative; sector rotation extends.

entries.name: A.O. Smith Corporation
entries.catalyst: 8-K dated 2026-06-22 disclosing officer/director departure or appointment
entries.thesis: A 5.02 officer change can cut either direction — planned retirement vs forced exit vs new strategic hire. The stock is rallying meaningfully on a day when industrials broadly are strong (XLI +1.2%), so part of the move is sector beta. Without reading the 8-K exhibit to determine whether this is a CEO succession, an activist-driven replacement, or a routine board change, the directional read isn't articulable at conf 3+.

entries.name: Builders FirstSource, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Housing-policy news flow (Trump housing bill discussion, Save America Act); macro risk-on in cyclicals
entries.thesis: BLDR's double-digit move aligns with the Trump housing-bill / Save America Act news cycle (housing affordability legislation discussion, possible interest rate pressure narrative) and broad industrial/small-cap risk-on (XLI +1.2%, IWM +0.5%). No 8-K, no earnings catalyst — this looks like macro positioning into a housing-policy event combined with sector rotation. Magnitude is large but matches the macro setup; no clear over/underreaction edge.
entries.what_kills: Single-stock unwind tomorrow with no follow-through from peers would suggest a squeeze rather than thesis, but doesn't constitute a directional fade signal on its own

entries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources
entries.catalyst: 6/18 Reg FD 8-K of unclear content; ongoing met-coal commodity weakness
entries.thesis: AMR down nearly 5% today and ~12% over 5 days, with a 7.01 Reg FD disclosure filed last week. Reg FD items are catch-all — could be investor presentation, could be guidance update — and without the filing contents we cannot tell which. Coking coal pricing is also pressured by global steel demand uncertainty. Cannot construct a 3+ confidence call in either direction without reading the 6/18 8-K exhibit.
entries.what_kills: Filing turns out to be benign (conference presentation) — then today's selling is pure commodity tape and likely RATIONAL given met-coal spot weakness

entries.name: Beam Therapeutics
entries.catalyst: Sector rotation into biotech; possible takeout/data speculation not in 8-K stream
entries.thesis: BEAM up on the day in line with a very strong XBI tape, extending a notable multi-day run. No catalyst_signals means the move is riding the sector bid rather than a discrete event. Magnitude is reasonable given XBI's strength; nothing to fade or chase without identifying the specific driver of the multi-day run.
entries.what_kills: XBI rolls over; BEAM gives back gains faster than the sector
